**

**

Spiritual Disciplines Map

This document is where the map of spiritual formation resources is developed. This is part of the [The Narrow Way App Project](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RzO02ANwUpW58j7pTaGlVqNB9xPYp_t8ksNTYQA_ink/edit) which is testing ground for resources for Holy Habits.

The latest work (and the current action) is in developing '[The Dynamics of Spiritual Growth](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lTNeSvLPK9zM5XqMuCnx1_JeaSm596FdVkDy_mqXJpw/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.78y3wr69i3nw)' part of this document.

BACKGROUND 

Dan Burke has developed the Paradigm of AscentTM which can be app-ified. The Paradigm of Ascent has been explained in [an article on SpiritualDirection.com](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kI3FptoiVaZkgHMuoz0OKlAq601xII3fOVNTdGHoPVI/edit), in [an appendix of Dan Burke's book Finding Peace in the Storm](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1K-NqOngOgZ5PnYyP__04gxG61Kt87DuRGg97uKQw2UU/edit) and in [a course on Apostoliviae.org](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1X-wQzc5tCEpJ7X0ReZPFbZcMbjKfHg4R6JvkgpimkvA/edit?pli=1). Each of the components of the Paradigm of Ascent are accessible as formation in particular books, resources or may need to be created. Here is a summary of the disciplines explained.

1.  Yes to God

2.  Sacraments: Confession and the Eucharist

3.  Daily Prayer

4.  Ascesis

5.  Guide (Spiritual Direction)

6.  Compass (Plan of Love, Magisterium)

7.  Map (3 ways & Carmelite Spirituality)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Dan's explanation in Finding Peace provides a nice sequence to the various disciplines which is as follows.

1.  Mental Prayer (Into the Deep)

2.  Examen

3.  Plan of Love

4.  Regular Confession

5.  More frequent Eucharist

6.  Discernment of Spirits (Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits)

7.  Paradigm of Ascent

8.  Moral life as explained in the Catechism of the Catholic Church

9.  Rosary

10.  Ascesis

This is not a complete treatment of all the disciplines necessary to enter the illuminative way. This list includes more of them many of which would also be included in the Paradigm of Ascent.

1.  Scripture

1.  The Better Part by Fr Bartunek. This is the best Gospel reflection resource and could be incorporated into the app.

3.  Working out your root sin

1.  'Spiritual Self-Evaluation' chapter in Navigating the Interior Life (also published as Journey to God) by Dan Burke p.61-83

2.  The Seven Deadly Sins by Kevin Vost

3.  Rooting Out Hidden Faults: How the Particular Examen Conquers Sin by James F. Mcelhone, CSC

4.  Novena to Our Lady of Sorrows

5.  Ask your spouse/parents (both!)

5.  The Spiritual Map

1.  'The Three Ways of the Spiritual Life' in Navigating the Interior Life (also published as Journey to God) by Dan Burke p. 87-114. This includes the stages as explained by Jean-Baptiste Chautard in The Soul of the Apostolate.

2.  Chapter 6 The Teresian Mansions in The Fire Within  by Fr Thomas Dubay. I have a summary [here](https://opencat.org/book/fire-within). 

3.  The podcast, ["Interior Castles"](https://www.discerninghearts.com/catholic-podcasts/the-interior-castle-st-teresa-of-avila-with-dr-anthony-lilles/) on Discerning Hearts with Chris Mcgregor and Dr Anthony Lilles.

4.  The Interior Castles by St Teresa of Avila.

5.  The Devil in the Castle by Dan Burke.

7.  Overcoming your sin

1.  [Deeper Examination of Conscience and the Seven Deadly Sins](https://www.apostoliviae.org/exam) 

2.  [Confession - Its Fruitful Practice (With an Examination of Conscience)](https://tanbooks.com/books/confession-its-fruitful-practice-with-an-examination-of-conscience/)

3.   Free course on [overcoming habitual sin](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/135/habitual-sin) on [apostoliviae.org](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/153/mental-prayer-101)

9.  Discernment of Spirits

1.  Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits by Dan Burke

2.  Discernment of Spirits by Fr Timothy Gallagher

3.  Setting the Captives Free by Timothy Gallagher

4.  Spiritual Consolation by Timothy Gallagher

11.  Discerning God's Will

1.   I have a summary of Discerning God's Will which can be turned into an online tool.

2.  Discerning God's Will by Timothy Gallagher

13.  Contemplation

1.  The Fire Within  by Fr Thomas Dubay.

15.  Contemplative Aridity

1.  Fr Donald Haggerty is the expert here. He provides an excellent explanation of the criteria to discern the difference between desolation and aridity in his book, Saint John of the Cross: Master of Contemplation 

2.  Fr Donald Haggerty's book, Contemplative Provocations, explains the dark night of the senses from within.

3.  His other books, Contemplative Enigmas and The Contemplative Hunger are also excellent.

4.  The works of St John of the Cross, Spiritual Canticle, The Ascent of Mt Carmel, The Dark night of the Soul., etc.

17.  Inner Healing

1.  All of Bob Schucht's material is good. Eg Be Healed.

19.  Deliverance/ Spiritual Warfare

1.  Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits by Dan Burke

2.  Daily Perimeter prayer

3.  Daily Auxilium Christianorum

4.  We have just recorded a set of talks by Dan and Stephanie Burke on Spiritual Warfare and

5.  Many people need to go through a variety of prayers which the [Exorcism App](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.kreagerconsulting.cathex&hl=en&gl=US) provides for free, such as [Prayers to Break the Freemasonic Curse](https://www.catholicexorcism.org/deliverance-prayers-for-the-laity)

21.  The Eucharist

1.  Dominus Est - It is the Lord! by Bishop Athanasius Schneider

2.  Meditations before Mass by Fr Romano Guardini

3.  Devotional Journey Into the Mass by Christopher Carstens.

23.  The Rosary

1.  The Contemplative Rosary by Dan Burke and Connie Rossini

25.  Asceticism

1.  Penance, fasting etc are essential for late purgative way to continue to make progress. Not sure what the best resource is for this area.

More detailed resourcing of some of these disciplines

15 minutes of Mental prayer daily (Have you completed the '[Mental Prayer - The Way of Prayer in Apostoli Viae](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/153/mental-prayer-101)' course?)

Daily Examen ([Redemptive Examen](https://www.apostoliviae.org/resources/41660/the-redemptive-examen),   [Discerning Hearts Podcast](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qD1Bs6l-agw&list=PLqueiEKHoJed00griUP_Bw-jDzT_oVKFj))

Plan of Love (Have you completed the '[Plan of Love - Loving God and Others with Divine Power and Purpose](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/189/plan-of-love-loving-god-and-others-with-divine-purpose)' course?) 

Proficiency in discernment of spirits (Have you completed the '[Discernment of Spirits Mini-Course](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/54/lectio-divina)'?)

Analysis of root sin ( [Root Sin Workbook](https://spiritualdirection.com/root-sin-ebook-2024), or 7 Deadly Sins by Kevin Vost from [St Paul](https://stpaulmemphis.com/product/the-seven-deadly-sins/) or [cheapest](https://booko.us/9781622822348/The-Seven-Deadly-Sins-and-Their-Forty-Four-Death-Dealing-Daughters-A-Thomistic-Guide-to-Vanquishing-Vice-and-Sin))

Understanding of Ignatian Discernment.  ([Gallagher's Summary](https://www.discerninghearts.com/PDF/Discerning_the_Will_of_God-DISCERNING_HEARTS.pdf), [Rob's summary of Spiritual Director's Handbook](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1l9MlLOQX2LrgOcIud5ln0vqDMjGNEpwoSFdzfmXRrBc/edit), [Discerning Hearts podcast](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ISReQLg3Kek&list=PLqueiEKHoJed7UCmoUsOQM3wzY6gSOQvw) or read the book (From [St Paul](https://stpaulmemphis.com/product/discerning-the-will-of-god-ignatian-guide-to-christian-decision-making/) or [cheapest](https://booko.us/9780824524890/Discerning-the-Will-of-God-An-Ignatian-Guide-to-Christian-Decision-Making)).

Understanding of Second Week Discernment. (Read [the guide](https://www.discerninghearts.com/Gallagher/The_Text_of_the_Rules.pdf) (and possibly [the case scenarios](https://www.discerninghearts.com/Gallagher/The_Text_of_the_Rules.pdf)) and listen/watch [the podcast](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hpUktn0ivek&list=PLqueiEKHoJeeXoWtohGadYigi5VlWvGMf) or read the book (From [St Paul](https://stpaulmemphis.com/product/5655/) or [cheapest](https://booko.us/9780824524296/Spiritual-Consolation)).

The basic way this all works is through the foundation of mental prayer. Mental prayer is the most important of all the practices (presupposing regular confession and Eucharist) since it personalises God's relationship and allows Him to work more directly and intimately with the person. With an examen, plan of love (Holy Habits), and good spiritual reading progress starts to become sustained. St. Ignatius' rules for the discernment of spirits helps to kick desolation and abide in God's peace. It is then a matter of rooting out sin, mortal, then venial, then imperfections. As this occurs prayer becomes more affective. Penance, fasting would start to increase as a process of detachment occurs. This gradually gives way to more of a desire for God's will over all other desires. Gradually aridity may be experienced and the start of contemplative graces. While mental prayer is essential for progress through the purgative way, asceticism and penances helps sustain progress through the illuminative way. The focus of purgation is of the will to God's will in all things.

FORMATION STREAM

There is a course map like a technology tree you would find in a computer game like civilisation. For example:

![](data:image/png;base64,)

The image above is a very poor mockup. The image below is more how I imagine it to look. What are the best next choices of knowledge, disciplines to develop as we make progress? What knowledge, disciplines be in place before a particular resource should be available?

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Courses would be sequenced in complexity (similar to the 5 year reading plan), but all guides/courses would be available. A survey could be used to help the user decide which course they should do next. This could also be guided by the AI in Holy Habits.

Each course would be made up of content units.

1.  Each unit of content is about 8 minutes. Inch by inch is a cinch, yard by yard is too hard. The masses out there need baby steps. At the same time we allow for people to go at it as fast as they want. We also teach a sustainable pace so they don't bomb out. Practically speaking we work with a unit of content that takes about 7 minutes. (This amount of time may be tweaked and could also be varied depending on the level of formation, eg shorter for beginners and longer for advanced.

2.  Each piece of content is multimodal, ie it can be watched, listened to, read, summarised, applied, quizzed, discussed, cross referenced and/or improved.

1.  A video can be watched.

2.  The audio can be listened to.

3.  It can be read.

4.  There is a short tl;dr version which is the key content for the quiz.

5.  If it is practical, such as prayer, there is a task to be completed and checked off which is automatically integrated to the habit feature of the app.

6.  There is a quiz (maybe using [h5p interactives](https://h5p.org/content-types-and-applications) to assess their understanding of the content. These quizzes contribute to a certificate.

7.  The content can be discussed. This could be within a group or a wider audience. There is a question of moderation here.

8.  There are links to other articles, etc about that topic.

9.  The improved feature, allows for someone to suggest improvements. The specifics of this role will need to be properly discerned.

4.  Feedback. This is where some 'AI' might come in. There can be feedback about how many content units are covered. Is someone overdoing it? Do they need to be more consistent. These feedback points can be integrated into the app.

For example here is an example of the kind of steps that can be integrated into the app for Into the Deep [https://opencat.org/](https://opencat.org/itdg)it'd 

For now it is a matter of setting up a number of guides that can then start to be incorporated into the Holy Habits App.

SO FAR

We have the following guides (which still need to be checked):

[INTRODUCTION GUIDE](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RzRhB_VZ6BLm5d7n66q3QsfU6asa3Md1rQuHvyPcF5k/edit)

[INTO THE DEEP GUIDE](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qbNad_Lo6_lrlGbkVnRmZ63bC0ilRFAIFPj0sdh3PvU/edit)

It is suggested that the next guide be: [Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits Guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/12HLMs-Z-RqmAo9n35jIzaMVsP_yrATlzdC1Ec56m2mM/edit)

But what about other guides? What should the sequence be? What categories should there be?

I think there are four basic categories

1.  Prayer/Vocal/meditation/contemplation

2.  Examen/Discernment/God's will

3.  Morality/root sin/detachment/ascesis

4.  Accompaniment/ministry/service/Spiritual Direction

5.  Possibly a theology formation track, eg Attributes of God, Trinity, Incarnation, Anthropology, Revelation (including the Magisterium), Sacraments.

Rob Zaar has this [PRAYER COURSE EXPLANATION](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1N51dkPiWolDPiYpcmNTRSfAyv10X9x1oQHVBgf_mJ0Q/edit) doc which needs updating, but might have some useful information. He also created this document: [The Narrow Way v3](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ee7_HS3MJj2UsFhVZgT1Cx5Tfv5xGTo6RS6SZ5e_YG4/edit) which might be useful. 

THE DYNAMICS OF SPIRITUAL GROWTH

As a way to move forward and in an attempt to expose the thinking that goes into developing a sequence of resources, the following explanations are provided. It is expected that more experienced and wiser people will share their wisdom to improve the following dynamics.

1.  Audience:

1.  Manageable: Inch by inch is a cinch, yard by yard is too hard. The system needs to be in steps that are small enough so those who are weaker can cope, but can be traversed quickly by those more capable.

2.  Background: Assume your audience knows nothing and is starting from the absolute beginning, but provide a fast track for those who do know. Each person has "an infinite capacity for self-delusion". Many people have read and know the meaning of words and spiritual practices, but that does not mean they are actually practicing them. Knowing about what mental prayer is and practicing mental prayer are two different things. Knowing about Jesus and knowing Jesus are two different things. Having the practice of vocal prayer and praying vocal prayers are two different things. Each module is written in two interconnected ways. The first is a complete explanation for a beginner. The second is a kind of content questionnaire that checks if someone actually understands the content with connections to where it is explained.

3.  Structure: The system is structured. A modular education model is used since it provides a way to ensure practical application as a measure of progress. Accountability is crucial for spiritual growth. The backdrop to this system is the Holy Habits app which is an accountability system. Many people will be first mansion and accountability is often the factor that helps someone break from habitual mortal sin. This is a hidden problem for many which requires a robust framework that with personal accompaniment will eventually bring victory in this area. For some, only such a robust focused methodology coupled with an openness to the Holy Spirit and personal accountability will engender the sustained focussed effort for victory. It will be the feedback from practical application which will improve this system so it will have the flexibility to meet divergent needs and the rigor to provide the necessary focus.

1.  Modular: The spiritual life is broken into various disciplines which are covered in discrete modules. Divide and conquer. Practically speaking, there are already discrete topics such as mental prayer, discernment, ascesis, root sin. Each should be tackled. Dealing with one topic at a time is part of making it manageable, which is explained later.

2.  Practical: The content is geared towards practical application. It is this practical application that is the core of the system. It allows for accountability and requires consolidation before progress. This is through checkboxes within the content to check if the person has completed or is practicing the suggestion.

3.  Classified: Modules are classed into streams and levels of depth. 

4.  Sequential: There is a general sequence to the modules.

5.  Consolidation: Modules suggest practical application which needs to be mastered to a degree before progress to another module.

6.  Recommendations: Once one module is mastered, a set of suggested modules are offered with a recommended module, but all modules can be accessed.

7.  Reinforcement: If a spiritual discipline is not sufficiently mastered, while progress is not made in breadth, there should be modules provided that will reinforce the topic to build up what is required to bring victory in that area. For example, a guy has a porn addiction and so a variety of disciplines (eg vocal prayer, reconciliation, mental prayer, accountability, renouncing lies, avoiding the near occasions of sin, etc) are needed to eventually bring victory in this area rather than progressing onto other topics eg the Interior Castles, virtues and vices, desolation vs aridity. 

8.  Multimedia: Module content can be accessed in a variety of ways, text, booklet, podcast, video, powerpoint for group presentation.

9.  Interactive: There are tools to provide interactivity (eg [h5p](https://h5p.org/content-types-and-applications)) to reinforce learning. 

10.  Multitrack: Content has two basic tracks through the content. The first is for those not familiar or practicing the content. This is the normal content with builtin checkboxes to check for practical application. The second is for those who think they already practice it and is in questionnaire form to check to see if they are actually practicing the discipline correctly. It is possible to put together the parts of the first track needed to cover what is lacking. 

5.  Content: Now we get into the actual sequence of spiritual disciplines. While there are many, we will focus on the most essential ones for now to get the ball rolling and to have a firm foundation upon which to add the other disciplines the right way.

1.  Vocal prayer:

**

**

Spiritual Disciplines Map

This document is where the map of spiritual formation resources is developed. This is part of the [The Narrow Way App Project](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RzO02ANwUpW58j7pTaGlVqNB9xPYp_t8ksNTYQA_ink/edit) which is testing ground for resources for Holy Habits.

The latest work (and the current action) is in developing '[The Dynamics of Spiritual Growth](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lTNeSvLPK9zM5XqMuCnx1_JeaSm596FdVkDy_mqXJpw/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.78y3wr69i3nw)' part of this document.

BACKGROUND 

Dan Burke has developed the Paradigm of AscentTM which can be app-ified. The Paradigm of Ascent has been explained in [an article on SpiritualDirection.com](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kI3FptoiVaZkgHMuoz0OKlAq601xII3fOVNTdGHoPVI/edit), in [an appendix of Dan Burke's book Finding Peace in the Storm](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1K-NqOngOgZ5PnYyP__04gxG61Kt87DuRGg97uKQw2UU/edit) and in [a course on Apostoliviae.org](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1X-wQzc5tCEpJ7X0ReZPFbZcMbjKfHg4R6JvkgpimkvA/edit?pli=1). Each of the components of the Paradigm of Ascent are accessible as formation in particular books, resources or may need to be created. Here is a summary of the disciplines explained.

1.  Yes to God

2.  Sacraments: Confession and the Eucharist

3.  Daily Prayer

4.  Ascesis

5.  Guide (Spiritual Direction)

6.  Compass (Plan of Love, Magisterium)

7.  Map (3 ways & Carmelite Spirituality)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Dan's explanation in Finding Peace provides a nice sequence to the various disciplines which is as follows.

1.  Mental Prayer (Into the Deep)

2.  Examen

3.  Plan of Love

4.  Regular Confession

5.  More frequent Eucharist

6.  Discernment of Spirits (Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits)

7.  Paradigm of Ascent

8.  Moral life as explained in the Catechism of the Catholic Church

9.  Rosary

10.  Ascesis

This is not a complete treatment of all the disciplines necessary to enter the illuminative way. This list includes more of them many of which would also be included in the Paradigm of Ascent.

1.  Scripture

1.  The Better Part by Fr Bartunek. This is the best Gospel reflection resource and could be incorporated into the app.

3.  Working out your root sin

1.  'Spiritual Self-Evaluation' chapter in Navigating the Interior Life (also published as Journey to God) by Dan Burke p.61-83

2.  The Seven Deadly Sins by Kevin Vost

3.  Rooting Out Hidden Faults: How the Particular Examen Conquers Sin by James F. Mcelhone, CSC

4.  Novena to Our Lady of Sorrows

5.  Ask your spouse/parents (both!)

5.  The Spiritual Map

1.  'The Three Ways of the Spiritual Life' in Navigating the Interior Life (also published as Journey to God) by Dan Burke p. 87-114. This includes the stages as explained by Jean-Baptiste Chautard in The Soul of the Apostolate.

2.  Chapter 6 The Teresian Mansions in The Fire Within  by Fr Thomas Dubay. I have a summary [here](https://opencat.org/book/fire-within). 

3.  The podcast, ["Interior Castles"](https://www.discerninghearts.com/catholic-podcasts/the-interior-castle-st-teresa-of-avila-with-dr-anthony-lilles/) on Discerning Hearts with Chris Mcgregor and Dr Anthony Lilles.

4.  The Interior Castles by St Teresa of Avila.

5.  The Devil in the Castle by Dan Burke.

7.  Overcoming your sin

1.  [Deeper Examination of Conscience and the Seven Deadly Sins](https://www.apostoliviae.org/exam) 

2.  [Confession - Its Fruitful Practice (With an Examination of Conscience)](https://tanbooks.com/books/confession-its-fruitful-practice-with-an-examination-of-conscience/)

3.   Free course on [overcoming habitual sin](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/135/habitual-sin) on [apostoliviae.org](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/153/mental-prayer-101)

9.  Discernment of Spirits

1.  Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits by Dan Burke

2.  Discernment of Spirits by Fr Timothy Gallagher

3.  Setting the Captives Free by Timothy Gallagher

4.  Spiritual Consolation by Timothy Gallagher

11.  Discerning God's Will

1.   I have a summary of Discerning God's Will which can be turned into an online tool.

2.  Discerning God's Will by Timothy Gallagher

13.  Contemplation

1.  The Fire Within  by Fr Thomas Dubay.

15.  Contemplative Aridity

1.  Fr Donald Haggerty is the expert here. He provides an excellent explanation of the criteria to discern the difference between desolation and aridity in his book, Saint John of the Cross: Master of Contemplation 

2.  Fr Donald Haggerty's book, Contemplative Provocations, explains the dark night of the senses from within.

3.  His other books, Contemplative Enigmas and The Contemplative Hunger are also excellent.

4.  The works of St John of the Cross, Spiritual Canticle, The Ascent of Mt Carmel, The Dark night of the Soul., etc.

17.  Inner Healing

1.  All of Bob Schucht's material is good. Eg Be Healed.

19.  Deliverance/ Spiritual Warfare

1.  Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits by Dan Burke

2.  Daily Perimeter prayer

3.  Daily Auxilium Christianorum

4.  We have just recorded a set of talks by Dan and Stephanie Burke on Spiritual Warfare and

5.  Many people need to go through a variety of prayers which the [Exorcism App](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.kreagerconsulting.cathex&hl=en&gl=US) provides for free, such as [Prayers to Break the Freemasonic Curse](https://www.catholicexorcism.org/deliverance-prayers-for-the-laity)

21.  The Eucharist

1.  Dominus Est - It is the Lord! by Bishop Athanasius Schneider

2.  Meditations before Mass by Fr Romano Guardini

3.  Devotional Journey Into the Mass by Christopher Carstens.

23.  The Rosary

1.  The Contemplative Rosary by Dan Burke and Connie Rossini

25.  Asceticism

1.  Penance, fasting etc are essential for late purgative way to continue to make progress. Not sure what the best resource is for this area.

More detailed resourcing of some of these disciplines

15 minutes of Mental prayer daily (Have you completed the '[Mental Prayer - The Way of Prayer in Apostoli Viae](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/153/mental-prayer-101)' course?)

Daily Examen ([Redemptive Examen](https://www.apostoliviae.org/resources/41660/the-redemptive-examen),   [Discerning Hearts Podcast](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qD1Bs6l-agw&list=PLqueiEKHoJed00griUP_Bw-jDzT_oVKFj))

Plan of Love (Have you completed the '[Plan of Love - Loving God and Others with Divine Power and Purpose](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/189/plan-of-love-loving-god-and-others-with-divine-purpose)' course?) 

Proficiency in discernment of spirits (Have you completed the '[Discernment of Spirits Mini-Course](https://www.apostoliviae.org/courses/54/lectio-divina)'?)

Analysis of root sin ( [Root Sin Workbook](https://spiritualdirection.com/root-sin-ebook-2024), or 7 Deadly Sins by Kevin Vost from [St Paul](https://stpaulmemphis.com/product/the-seven-deadly-sins/) or [cheapest](https://booko.us/9781622822348/The-Seven-Deadly-Sins-and-Their-Forty-Four-Death-Dealing-Daughters-A-Thomistic-Guide-to-Vanquishing-Vice-and-Sin))

Understanding of Ignatian Discernment.  ([Gallagher's Summary](https://www.discerninghearts.com/PDF/Discerning_the_Will_of_God-DISCERNING_HEARTS.pdf), [Rob's summary of Spiritual Director's Handbook](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1l9MlLOQX2LrgOcIud5ln0vqDMjGNEpwoSFdzfmXRrBc/edit), [Discerning Hearts podcast](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ISReQLg3Kek&list=PLqueiEKHoJed7UCmoUsOQM3wzY6gSOQvw) or read the book (From [St Paul](https://stpaulmemphis.com/product/discerning-the-will-of-god-ignatian-guide-to-christian-decision-making/) or [cheapest](https://booko.us/9780824524890/Discerning-the-Will-of-God-An-Ignatian-Guide-to-Christian-Decision-Making)).

Understanding of Second Week Discernment. (Read [the guide](https://www.discerninghearts.com/Gallagher/The_Text_of_the_Rules.pdf) (and possibly [the case scenarios](https://www.discerninghearts.com/Gallagher/The_Text_of_the_Rules.pdf)) and listen/watch [the podcast](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hpUktn0ivek&list=PLqueiEKHoJeeXoWtohGadYigi5VlWvGMf) or read the book (From [St Paul](https://stpaulmemphis.com/product/5655/) or [cheapest](https://booko.us/9780824524296/Spiritual-Consolation)).

The basic way this all works is through the foundation of mental prayer. Mental prayer is the most important of all the practices (presupposing regular confession and Eucharist) since it personalises God's relationship and allows Him to work more directly and intimately with the person. With an examen, plan of love (Holy Habits), and good spiritual reading progress starts to become sustained. St. Ignatius' rules for the discernment of spirits helps to kick desolation and abide in God's peace. It is then a matter of rooting out sin, mortal, then venial, then imperfections. As this occurs prayer becomes more affective. Penance, fasting would start to increase as a process of detachment occurs. This gradually gives way to more of a desire for God's will over all other desires. Gradually aridity may be experienced and the start of contemplative graces. While mental prayer is essential for progress through the purgative way, asceticism and penances helps sustain progress through the illuminative way. The focus of purgation is of the will to God's will in all things.

FORMATION STREAM

There is a course map like a technology tree you would find in a computer game like civilisation. For example:

![](data:image/png;base64,)

The image above is a very poor mockup. The image below is more how I imagine it to look. What are the best next choices of knowledge, disciplines to develop as we make progress? What knowledge, disciplines be in place before a particular resource should be available?

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Courses would be sequenced in complexity (similar to the 5 year reading plan), but all guides/courses would be available. A survey could be used to help the user decide which course they should do next. This could also be guided by the AI in Holy Habits.

Each course would be made up of content units.

1.  Each unit of content is about 8 minutes. Inch by inch is a cinch, yard by yard is too hard. The masses out there need baby steps. At the same time we allow for people to go at it as fast as they want. We also teach a sustainable pace so they don't bomb out. Practically speaking we work with a unit of content that takes about 7 minutes. (This amount of time may be tweaked and could also be varied depending on the level of formation, eg shorter for beginners and longer for advanced.

2.  Each piece of content is multimodal, ie it can be watched, listened to, read, summarised, applied, quizzed, discussed, cross referenced and/or improved.

1.  A video can be watched.

2.  The audio can be listened to.

3.  It can be read.

4.  There is a short tl;dr version which is the key content for the quiz.

5.  If it is practical, such as prayer, there is a task to be completed and checked off which is automatically integrated to the habit feature of the app.

6.  There is a quiz (maybe using [h5p interactives](https://h5p.org/content-types-and-applications) to assess their understanding of the content. These quizzes contribute to a certificate.

7.  The content can be discussed. This could be within a group or a wider audience. There is a question of moderation here.

8.  There are links to other articles, etc about that topic.

9.  The improved feature, allows for someone to suggest improvements. The specifics of this role will need to be properly discerned.

4.  Feedback. This is where some 'AI' might come in. There can be feedback about how many content units are covered. Is someone overdoing it? Do they need to be more consistent. These feedback points can be integrated into the app.

For example here is an example of the kind of steps that can be integrated into the app for Into the Deep [https://opencat.org/](https://opencat.org/itdg)it'd 

For now it is a matter of setting up a number of guides that can then start to be incorporated into the Holy Habits App.

SO FAR

We have the following guides (which still need to be checked):

[INTRODUCTION GUIDE](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RzRhB_VZ6BLm5d7n66q3QsfU6asa3Md1rQuHvyPcF5k/edit)

[INTO THE DEEP GUIDE](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qbNad_Lo6_lrlGbkVnRmZ63bC0ilRFAIFPj0sdh3PvU/edit)

It is suggested that the next guide be: [Spiritual Warfare and the Discernment of Spirits Guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/12HLMs-Z-RqmAo9n35jIzaMVsP_yrATlzdC1Ec56m2mM/edit)

But what about other guides? What should the sequence be? What categories should there be?

I think there are four basic categories

1.  Prayer/Vocal/meditation/contemplation

2.  Examen/Discernment/God's will

3.  Morality/root sin/detachment/ascesis

4.  Accompaniment/ministry/service/Spiritual Direction

5.  Possibly a theology formation track, eg Attributes of God, Trinity, Incarnation, Anthropology, Revelation (including the Magisterium), Sacraments.

Rob Zaar has this [PRAYER COURSE EXPLANATION](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1N51dkPiWolDPiYpcmNTRSfAyv10X9x1oQHVBgf_mJ0Q/edit) doc which needs updating, but might have some useful information. He also created this document: [The Narrow Way v3](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ee7_HS3MJj2UsFhVZgT1Cx5Tfv5xGTo6RS6SZ5e_YG4/edit) which might be useful. 

THE DYNAMICS OF SPIRITUAL GROWTH

As a way to move forward and in an attempt to expose the thinking that goes into developing a sequence of resources, the following explanations are provided. It is expected that more experienced and wiser people will share their wisdom to improve the following dynamics.

1.  Audience:

1.  Manageable: Inch by inch is a cinch, yard by yard is too hard. The system needs to be in steps that are small enough so those who are weaker can cope, but can be traversed quickly by those more capable.

2.  Background: Assume your audience knows nothing and is starting from the absolute beginning, but provide a fast track for those who do know. Each person has "an infinite capacity for self-delusion". Many people have read and know the meaning of words and spiritual practices, but that does not mean they are actually practicing them. Knowing about what mental prayer is and practicing mental prayer are two different things. Knowing about Jesus and knowing Jesus are two different things. Having the practice of vocal prayer and praying vocal prayers are two different things. Each module is written in two interconnected ways. The first is a complete explanation for a beginner. The second is a kind of content questionnaire that checks if someone actually understands the content with connections to where it is explained.

3.  Structure: The system is structured. A modular education model is used since it provides a way to ensure practical application as a measure of progress. Accountability is crucial for spiritual growth. The backdrop to this system is the Holy Habits app which is an accountability system. Many people will be first mansion and accountability is often the factor that helps someone break from habitual mortal sin. This is a hidden problem for many which requires a robust framework that with personal accompaniment will eventually bring victory in this area. For some, only such a robust focused methodology coupled with an openness to the Holy Spirit and personal accountability will engender the sustained focussed effort for victory. It will be the feedback from practical application which will improve this system so it will have the flexibility to meet divergent needs and the rigor to provide the necessary focus.

1.  Modular: The spiritual life is broken into various disciplines which are covered in discrete modules. Divide and conquer. Practically speaking, there are already discrete topics such as mental prayer, discernment, ascesis, root sin. Each should be tackled. Dealing with one topic at a time is part of making it manageable, which is explained later.

2.  Practical: The content is geared towards practical application. It is this practical application that is the core of the system. It allows for accountability and requires consolidation before progress. This is through checkboxes within the content to check if the person has completed or is practicing the suggestion.

3.  Classified: Modules are classed into streams and levels of depth. 

4.  Sequential: There is a general sequence to the modules.

5.  Consolidation: Modules suggest practical application which needs to be mastered to a degree before progress to another module.

6.  Recommendations: Once one module is mastered, a set of suggested modules are offered with a recommended module, but all modules can be accessed.

7.  Reinforcement: If a spiritual discipline is not sufficiently mastered, while progress is not made in breadth, there should be modules provided that will reinforce the topic to build up what is required to bring victory in that area. For example, a guy has a porn addiction and so a variety of disciplines (eg vocal prayer, reconciliation, mental prayer, accountability, renouncing lies, avoiding the near occasions of sin, etc) are needed to eventually bring victory in this area rather than progressing onto other topics eg the Interior Castles, virtues and vices, desolation vs aridity. 

8.  Multimedia: Module content can be accessed in a variety of ways, text, booklet, podcast, video, powerpoint for group presentation.

9.  Interactive: There are tools to provide interactivity (eg [h5p](https://h5p.org/content-types-and-applications)) to reinforce learning. 

10.  Multitrack: Content has two basic tracks through the content. The first is for those not familiar or practicing the content. This is the normal content with builtin checkboxes to check for practical application. The second is for those who think they already practice it and is in questionnaire form to check to see if they are actually practicing the discipline correctly. It is possible to put together the parts of the first track needed to cover what is lacking. 

5.  Content: Now we get into the actual sequence of spiritual disciplines. While there are many, we will focus on the most essential ones for now to get the ball rolling and to have a firm foundation upon which to add the other disciplines the right way.

1.  Vocal prayer:

**

**

TNW Guide Writing guidelines

BACKGROUND

Currently there are two guides written, though the first is introductory. 

[INTRODUCTION GUIDE](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RzRhB_VZ6BLm5d7n66q3QsfU6asa3Md1rQuHvyPcF5k/edit)

[INTO THE DEEP GUIDE](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qbNad_Lo6_lrlGbkVnRmZ63bC0ilRFAIFPj0sdh3PvU/edit)

And a third that needs to be written. Of course, over time, there will be many more guides to be written over time once these have been identified as the [Spiritual Disciplines Map](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lTNeSvLPK9zM5XqMuCnx1_JeaSm596FdVkDy_mqXJpw/edit) is refined.  

[Spiritual Disciplines Map](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lTNeSvLPK9zM5XqMuCnx1_JeaSm596FdVkDy_mqXJpw/edit)

Reading and using them will help get the idea of what works and what could work better.

This writing guide will be a combined effort of pedagogues and writers since it incorporates skills of both.

WRITING GUIDE

These points are just to get the ball rolling.

1.  The general key principle is to extract from the text, eg Into the Deep practical steps that a reader should put into practice, eg create a prayer corner. These will be checkboxes.

2.  The guide should be broken down into parts that take about 7-8 minutes to read which itself can be a checkbox. This allows the guide to be gradually digested and is achievable (theoretically) by anyone on a day to day basis.

3.  There can be a variety of tasks

1.  Some are single tasks, eg read pages 20-24.

2.  Some tasks might take time, eg create a prayer corner.

3.  Some tasks might be daily tasks, eg pray 10 minutes a day.

4.  Some tasks might be part of a review, eg how is the resolution part of mental prayer going.

5.  Any other thoughts?

5.  There can be a variety of styles to the same resource, eg it would be written differently for young adults compared to single mothers.

6.  How should it be aimed? How persuasively? Should be hooks? Quotes? Encouragingly? 

7.  How much background should be included? How much technical language? A glossary?

8.  What level of background knowledge should be assumed?

9.  Any other questions points included?

**

**

THEOLOGY PROBATIONARY GUILD

Welcome to the Theology Group. This is for probationary theologians. A proficient theologian is someone who has taken the oath of fidelity to the Magisterium and is able to check a document for magisterial fidelity. This means the person knows their way around the Catholic Catechism, Ott and Denzinger. They know enough to know what they know and what they don't know, ie will look up or ask others any questions they are unsure of.

We will be developing a quiz and course to support the formation of possible theologians.

**

**

TRANSLATORS GUILD

Welcome translators. 

One project is to translate the various Narrow Way app guides into other languages. But it's not worth starting that until we have a stable version of one of the guides.

The other task that could be approved is to translate Into the Deep into other languages. So far it's only been translated into Spanish.

There may be various tasks ending up in the Assets list in this group.

**

**

GATEKEEPERS GUILD

Gatekeepers check new projects, docs, resources to see if they should proceed. If not they should send a message back to the initiator explaining why. This group could be called shepherds as well. It is the best group to allow new admins to join and be trained up.

**


**

CREATIVES GUILD

Welcome creatives. I am sure you can make this space look a lot better!

We want all our work to be beautiful. Feel free to make any suggestions to make what we do beautiful and thus give greater glory to God.

Possible current tasks is creating [a logo for the Narrow Way App and project](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1P_4szFhS05qkkAJccIVZNDPX5FiDtUUcqdCv2SuUsDU/edit).

Another task is working on the [User Interface (UI)](https://docs.google.com/document/d/11UEJLm0c3JUpxKR1xtW-iICx5JajausM5GHdcrjFJbY/edit) of the Narrow Way App. 

There will be other tasks as time goes on.

**

VIDEO EDITORS GUILD

Hi video editors. This group is not just about video editing, but all things video in AV Commons.

The main project is: [Video Database Project](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1iS9HDROPPAGFE8EAafwy-hhvSAW1kitiTUZQlawHjC0/edit)

There may be various projects passed onto this group over time.

**


**

Video Database Project

1.  VISION

The Video Database Project (VDP) has three aims

1.  To create a central database of all AV videos.

2.  To create a search feature for that database.

3.  To share the repository of that database as public domain.

4.  The best explanations for each topic/discipline can be identified and integrated into [The Narrow Way App](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RzO02ANwUpW58j7pTaGlVqNB9xPYp_t8ksNTYQA_ink/edit). These videos should be modified according to [best practice](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1JHL9NuEbI7dnt3G4Q1DOJC0mtCJ2ZcXCGtTR7t5bz4g/edit#bookmark=id.wx3zpynyfuku).

2.  ASSETS

All AV videos. Mainly in Vimeo

| ID | Name (linked) | Description | Process | Due |
| --- |  --- |  --- |  --- |  --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |

3.  TASKS

1.  Decide on what kind of database to use. For now a Google sheet could be used so multiple people can contribute to the project.

2.  Ubuntu has a downloading tool that can be used to create a repository of all the videos. There could be several resolutions used. 

3.  Premier Pro has more accurate transcription than youtube (and I presume vimeo). It would be great if the coders group could work out a way to auto transcribe all the videos and put the raw transcript into the database.

4.  Using Natural Language Processing tools (NLP tools also known as AI) the key words for each video can then be auto extracted and used as search terms in the search engine.

5.  A website could be created to be a front end for the database search engine. This could then have the url search.apostoliviae.org for example.

6.  Using NLP tools to cross map all the spiritual disciplines (from [the map](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lTNeSvLPK9zM5XqMuCnx1_JeaSm596FdVkDy_mqXJpw/edit)) to the video database. Then the best explanations for each discipline be turned into short educational videos for each key concept according to [video education best practice](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1JHL9NuEbI7dnt3G4Q1DOJC0mtCJ2ZcXCGtTR7t5bz4g/edit#bookmark=id.wx3zpynyfuku).

7.  These videos are then embedded into The Narrow Way App.

4.  MEMBERS

The video editors group will be responsible for this project, but obviously supported by other groups, particularly coders..

5.  OTHER

Anything else that does not fit into the other categories.
